time.passTimeHint: Let time pass to the next part of the day

opening.subtitle: Eurbanities and Fragmentation
opening.selectHint: Who walks into the assembly tonight?
opening.name: Name

report.printHint: Printable report (print or save as PDF)
report.popupBlocked: The report window was blocked — allow pop-ups and retry.
report.title: Justurbanities — Educational report

debrief.institutions_vs_participation: Distrust in institutions vs trust in participation

slot.data: A data set left home (empty slot)

offline.title: Offline play
offline.unsupported: Cache API not available in this browser.

logbook.title: Logbook — Promises
logbook.empty: No promises yet. They are made in conversation.

assembly.title: Assembly of Just Futures
assembly.notReady: The assembly has not been called yet. The city is still living its week.

prep.title: Preparation — what you bring to the table

room.title: Phase 1 — Who is in the room?

tone.cold: all numbers — the room goes cold

done.title: The pact is signed
done.overpromise: The plan promises more than the city has — it will weigh on the ending.
done.withinMeans: The plan stays within the city's means.

mp.title: Classroom session
mp.invalid: Check the code (6 characters, no 0/O/1/I) and your name.

facilitator.title: Facilitator — session dashboard
facilitator.loadFailed: Could not load the session log — check the connection and retry.

start.text: You made it, {playerName}. Have a look around — there's a lot of history on these walls. And a few cracks, if you look closely.

choices.alive: “It's good to see the Center still alive.”

noticeboard.text: (The board lists twelve events this month. Average attendance: four.)

assembly.text: I'm preparing a new citizens' assembly. The Center works… for those who reach it — the point is the whole city that never comes through that door.

start.text: Three stops skipped this morning. Half the people who'd like to come to meetings simply can't get here — it's not apathy, it's geography.

choices.assembly_mind: “I'll prepare the assembly with this in mind too.”

map.text: (Ben opens the district map: three crosses where the bus never came, one underpass with steps, a crossing too narrow for wheels.)

start.text: The Crossroads. Buses, stalls, shortcuts and queues: this is where the district decides who arrives, who waits and who gives up.
Main Bus Hub. Three lines by day, one after dinner. The timetable assumes nobody works evenings.

noted.text: You write it down. Evidence is how a complaint becomes a proposal.

start.text: The market is the district's living room: half the stalls take cash only, and the vendors know everyone's name.
A narrow crossing between parked cars. Fine on foot, a puzzle with a stroller, impossible in a wheelchair. Ben would have measured it already.
Civic Info Point. Opening hours: shorter than the queue. Still, the noticeboard is honest work.
Three interviews, three districts, no solutions in your pocket. If you come back with a proposal instead of three stories, you've done the wrong mission.

choices.engage: I'll go and listen. To the silences too.
choices.shortcut: I've already talked to people, I know what's needed.

resolution.text: (leafing through the empathy maps) Look: three neighbourhoods, three different ways of saying “nobody ever comes back”. That's where we start.

start.text: That crossing blocks children, older people and anyone on wheels — wheelchair or pram. The Council “will assess”; I've stopped waiting for verbs in the future tense.

choices.engage: We do the temporary fix AND the real proposal.
choices.shortcut: I'll report the problem to the technical office and be done.

resolution.text: (testing the ramp) It holds. And the difference between today and a ticket is that tonight Lia crosses on her own.

start.text: I can give you a photo with the mayoral sash, or a mandate with delegated powers and a budget. The first is free; the second you have to make defensible in council.

choices.engage: Here are names, dates and reviews.
choices.shortcut: Let's hold the event, the rest will sort itself out.

resolution.text: (signing) Know this: skip one review and the council will throw it in my face for years. (smiles) Don't skip it.

start.text: Thirty years ago that pier was one long party; now you want to “regenerate” it. Regenerate — conjugated from the verb to forget. (theatrical pause) Convince me otherwise.

choices.engage: Tell me about it. We put it inside the project, not in place of the project.

resolution.text: (at the dinner, moved) All right, all right. If memory serves to open the future instead of barring it… then growing old has been of some use.

start.text: One Saturday, twenty hands, zero money. Courtyard 17 can become a common room — or stay the place where the Corporate hangs his rendering: pick the Saturday.

choices.engage: I'll organise the crews and materials.
choices.shortcut: We'll call in a firm, sooner or later.

resolution.text: (wiping her hands) It's not finished — common things never are, you maintain them. That's the beauty of them.

start.text: I've got two drafts: “THE USUAL SUSPECTS' CENTER” — twelve thousand guaranteed clicks — or the true story, which is slower and less sexy. My editor wants the first by tonight; give me the second.

choices.engage: Here are the accounts, the photos and three people to interview. Check everything.
choices.shortcut: Write whatever you want, I don't have time.

resolution.text: (showing the piece) “Participation isn't dead: it changed address.” Fewer clicks, more true. (sighs) I hate it when you people improve me.

start.text: A hundred rumours pass through here every day — eighty true, twenty poisonous — and I sort them by ear. Help me turn this into a proper information point, without switching off the chatter: the chatter is half the neighbourhood.

choices.engage: A verified noticeboard, five languages, and your ear stays editor-in-chief.
choices.shortcut: A sign with the Council's opening hours will do.

resolution.text: (arranging the noticeboard) Look: notices in Urdu, Polish and Italian, and people's little slips underneath, same as ever. Order and warmth — like tea.

start.text: Everyone asks me to “turn a blind eye” — I have two, and a rulebook. But there is Article 9, collaboration pacts: nobody ever uses it because it takes work… fancy some?

choices.engage: We write the pact properly, clause by clause.
choices.shortcut: We just open it, we'll see later.

resolution.text: (stamping) Collaboration Pact no. 1. (almost smiling) Did you know that rules, used well, are quite moving?

start.text: The last usable run out of Hill Gardens is 19:40; whoever works the evening shift walks home, forty minutes, in the dark. I've had the numbers in my head for years — nobody's ever asked me for them.

choices.engage: I'm asking. Let's count a week of runs together.

resolution.text: (dossier on the dashboard) Twenty-three people on foot every night — now it's in writing. Let's see what weighs more at that table: my odometer or their Excel sheet.

start.text: I've done the sums: twelve active volunteers, four on the edge of burnout — if one collapses, everyone's shifts collapse. Caring for those who care isn't a luxury: it's maintenance of the network.

choices.engage: Shifts with mandatory rest, pairs, and the right to say no.
choices.shortcut: They're adults, they can pace themselves.

resolution.text: (pinning up the rota) “Resting is participating.” Piotr suggested that line — he's back.

start.text: Look at my bar: the pensioners' table, the kids' table, the newcomers' table — three islands. Same walls, zero words between them; I've got the space, what I'm missing is the excuse.

choices.engage: Theme nights and a shared noticeboard. We build the excuse together.
choices.shortcut: Put up a sign saying 'talk to each other'.

resolution.text: (gesturing at the room) Thursday: backgammon tournament, Donald versus the Youth Court kids. He lost and wants a rematch. (under his breath) When false rumours go round now, they fall apart on their own in here: people know each other.

start.text: Pots in the windows, a painted bench, an adopted flowerbed — small, yes. But a tended place says “someone is staying here”; what matters is that it stays in the residents' hands and doesn't become a shop window for tourists.

choices.engage: Minimal green, co-tended, with the names of those who look after it.
choices.shortcut: We'll call a garden centre and be done.

resolution.text: (watering with a little girl) There are three rules: who plants, tends; who tends, signs; who signs, tells the story. Beauty without keepers is only scenery.

start.text: You adults measure streets in metres; I measure them in “where I can go on my own”. Want to see my map? It's full of holes.

choices.engage: You lead. I'll write down everything you say.

resolution.text: (at the noticeboard, showing the pinned-up map) That red X — I put it there. And they copied it into the real document. (to Zoe) See? Sometimes they do listen to us.

start.text: Everyone talks about the climate in the future tense; I have Lake Edge's drains full of leaves now, and a list of low houses that go under after two hours of rain. Prevention is boring — that's why nobody does it.

choices.engage: A channel-clearing day + evacuation list + assembly point. Boring stuff — let's do it.
choices.shortcut: If it happens, we'll call civil protection.

resolution.text: (watching the sky) Now we're even allowed to hope it won't be needed. Hoping AFTER you've cleared the channels is a different kind of hope altogether.

start.text: I taught for forty years; now I watch intelligent adults sit silent in meetings for fear of a grammar mistake. Language is the city's first door — I still have the key, if anyone is interested.

choices.engage: A language and citizenship course. And your former pupils as tutors.
choices.shortcut: There are apps for learning languages.

resolution.text: (after the first lesson) Mrs Anh asked a question in public today. Wrong subjunctive, perfect courage. (adjusting her glasses) We shall correct the subjunctive.

start.text: The papers only ever cover Hill Gardens in the crime pages; I can draw, and the kids at the wall know what needs drawing — it's called counter-narrative. But if I'm the only one signing it, that's just another theft.

choices.engage: Co-creation: the residents choose the stories, you draw them, you sign together.
choices.shortcut: You do it, you're the artist.

resolution.text: (in front of the mural signed by 14 hands) My name is the smallest one. I've never been this happy about a bad placement.

start.text: I deliver even where the doorbells have lost their names. Mr Ferro, third floor — I'm the only voice he hears all week, and I know twenty more like him: no list holds them.

choose.text: Here they are: older people alone, newcomers. What do we do?

choices.connect: Then we make the list ourselves. Discreetly, and with a face for every name.

resolution.text: (nearly weeping) Mr Ferro made coffee for two neighbours. Twenty-three years without opening his door to anyone — twenty-three! (blows his nose) Excuse me, it's the dust.

start.text: (flat voice, contract already printed) Full refurbishment, ninety days, twenty-year management to us — the neighbourhood gets a beautiful space, and the use, well… gets regulated. Sign here.

choices.accept: Sign the contract. Ninety days, a beautiful space.
choices.negotiate: Countersign the civic pact: guaranteed public hours, social tariffs, an annual review.
choices.refuse: We already have ninety neighbours. We don't need ninety days.

resolution.text: (closing the briefcase, without rancour) Interesting. Tired cities usually sign. (at the door) We'll be back when you're tired.

start.text: See Piotr? Third volunteer shift this week — nobody asked him, it's just that nobody else steps up. (lowering her voice) Participation that burns people out isn't participation.
“Same old club, same old faces” — eighty-four comments. I'm writing a piece on the participation crisis; all I need is the right headline.

choices.other_story: “What if the story were a different one?”

start.text: There are at least four empty spaces between Grey Yards and Old Blocks — good walls, good bones. What's missing is keys, permits and people with time: three things no assembly has ever produced.
At my bar I hear things nobody in here would ever say. If you want to understand the city, come by — the espresso is more honest than the minutes.

(On the Center's screen, a message from the Town Hall: “Consultation on the neighbourhoods opening. We need concrete results, not another document. — A.S.”) Elena, participation office. I'll be honest from the start: there are no funds for everything, and I won't sign promises we can't keep.

choices.clear_limit: “Better a clear limit than an empty promise.”

start.text: …and here Lake Edge, where— (the projected map GLITCHES: lines between neighbourhoods snap, colours drop, contradictory notifications overlap) It's not the first time. The technicians say the map is fine — it's as if it were the city that no longer holds together. RUBEN (snapping a photo): “Eurbania is fragmenting. There's my headline.”

invite.text: Assembly, Thursday. But this time no “open doors and let's see who turns up” — I want to know who doesn't come, and why.

choices.accept: “Thursday. I'll be there — and I'll notice who isn't.”

shift.text: 17:00. The shift is over — almost. The boss leans in: “Twenty minutes of overtime? Just today.” The assembly is at six; Zoe comes out of after-school at half past.

choices.accept: Accept. Twenty minutes won't kill anyone.

bus.text: At the stop, GWEN from the window: “The 14's skipping this run — breakdown, they say. Next one's in twenty minutes, or you cut through the underpass.”

choices.underpass: The underpass. (Dark, stepped — a neighbour with a pram turns back. Maya notes it down.)
choices.call_farah: Call Farah: “Could you pick Zoe up from school?” (She hates asking.)

zoe.text: The phone rings. ZOE: “I forgot my recorder at school and Miss checks tomorrow. If I don't fetch it she'll tell me off. Can you, please please—”

choices.go_back: Go back to the school. Fifteen minutes more.
choices.tomorrow: “We'll explain to your teacher tomorrow.” (Zoe sulks.)
choices.farah_again: Ask Farah for this too. (The first care network switches on.)

dilemma.text: 18:05. The Center's lights are on down the street. Zoe's hand is warm and the day weighs a ton.

choices.walk_in: Walk in late, Zoe by the hand.
choices.online: Follow online from the playground — present by half.
choices.give_up: Give up, but text Anna: “Hold the meetings when people who work can come.”

invitation.text: “The S.V. is hereby invited to attend the consultative session pursuant to Art. 12…” SAMIR (to himself): “S.V.”? Is that me, or someone else? (to a workmate) You going? WORKMATE: Me? That stuff's not for us — they talk, they put it in the minutes, the end.

choices.ask_abdullah: Ask Abdullah what it actually means. (“Come. If you feel out of place, you sit next to me.”)
choices.write_anna: Write to Anna for clarification. (She apologises for the language.)
choices.ignore: Ignore the invitation. The city will take you there anyway.

to_crossing.text: Delivery run first, then the Hub. The fastest way cuts through the Narrow Crossing — it always has.

choices.ride: Get on the bike. (Head to the Crossroads: the crossing awaits.)

fence.text: You SLAM to a stop. A construction fence has sealed the Narrow Crossing overnight — no sign, no alternative route. On the other side, an older man turns his trolley around.

choices.kids_gap: Look for the gap the kids use. (You get through — and see exactly who can't.)
choices.photo: Photograph it and mark it on the map. (The first lived-map entry.)
choices.detour: Take the long detour on the busy road.

checkpoint.text: Outside the Town Hall, an attendant raises a hand — at you, not at the man in a suit who walked in first. “ID and reason for your visit?”

choices.show_invitation: Show the invitation. (Abdullah appears behind you: “He's with me.”)
choices.show_invitation_alone: Show the invitation. (He studies it a beat too long. You get in; it costs.)
choices.ask_everyone: “Do you ask everyone, or just me?”

dilemma.text: The assembly hall hums behind the door. Three barriers between home and this room — you counted them.

choices.sit_back: Go in and sit at the back, in silence.
choices.name_barriers: Go in and name the barriers: fence, underpass, checkpoint.
choices.name_barriers_bridge: Go in and name the barriers — Abdullah nods you forward.
choices.hand_photo: Don't go in. Hand Anna the photo of the fence at the door.

meeting.text: The pre-assembly meeting never ends. TOM: “If you promise to open the spaces without the safety commission, the liability is yours. Signed: yours.”

site_visit.text: Two hours before the assembly. Grey Yards is ten minutes off the route — the courtyard she will be talking about is there, behind a padlock. SIGRID is measuring the entrance in hand-spans: “Three days' work and this place is a common room. Three years, if we wait for a funding call.”

choices.no_promises: “I can't promise anything.” — and listen anyway. (“Better than the ones who promise and vanish.”)
choices.skip: No time. Straight to the assembly, slides ready.

ruben.text: RUBEN, recorder out: “One statement: does the Council believe in this process, or is it the usual shop window?”

choices.verifiable_dates: “I believe in it. Judge the Council by its actions — I'll give you verifiable dates.”

dilemma.text: Her turn to speak. The folder holds the real numbers — smaller than anyone hopes.

choices.state_limits: State the budget limits at once, figures in hand.
choices.technical: Full technical presentation. (Precise and cold — someone whispers “she talks like a PDF”.)
choices.technical_saved: Full presentation — but she opens with the padlock, not the slides.

closing_time.text: 17:30. The rush hour is the till's best friend, and the assembly starts at six. The delivery is late, again.

choices.close_early: Close early. The till will forgive — maybe.
choices.ask_neighbour: Ask the shopkeeper next door to cover the last hour.
choices.stay_open: Stay open and send a message: “With you in spirit.”

email.text: New email. URBAN RENEWAL PARTNERS: “Dear proprietor, your premises fall within an area of interest to us. We propose an introductory meeting, with no obligation.”

choices.forward_amin: Forward it to Amin: “Know anything about this?” (“Third shop this month. They buy where the neighbourhood is tired.”)

amin.text: AMIN, drying a cup: “Come to the assembly — if you're there, shopkeepers stop being ‘the ones who never come’. And bring something: people think better on a full stomach.”

choices.bring_goods: Load crates of water and fruit.
choices.just_words: “Assemblies are just words.” (“So are quotes, until you sign.”)

dilemma.text: 17:55. The shutter is half-way down. In one pocket the shop keys, in the other the invitation — and that email.

choices.investor_meeting: Go to the investor meeting instead of the assembly.
choices.keep_shop: Don't go. Keep the shop open.

vignettes.text: On the way to the Center the city shows you four scenes at once: Maya checking the timetable with Zoe's school folder underarm; Samir stopped at a fence that wasn't there yesterday; Elena juggling a case file and a paper coffee cup; Luca half-lowering his shutter with crates at his feet. You can't be everywhere.

choices.with_maya: Wait for the bus with Maya and keep an eye on Zoe.
choices.with_elena: Offer Elena a coffee and one honest question.
choices.with_luca: Give Luca a hand loading the crates.
choices.observe: Go straight to the assembly and observe everything.

who.text: The Community Center, 18:20. Whoever arrived sits in the circle; whoever follows online is a thumbnail on a screen; whoever is missing is an empty chair with a name. ANNA: “Before we talk about projects, let's look at ourselves: who's here? And who's missing — and why?”

choices.look: (Look around the circle: the first “Who is in the room”.)

ben.text: BEN: “Some chairs being empty is not a detail. It's THE data point.”

choices.elena_concrete: (Elena asks to speak — she saw the courtyard today.)

elena_saw.text: ELENA: “I saw the Grey Yards courtyard today — three days' work, Sigrid says. I'm checking what it takes to give her the keys.” (The circle leans in: this isn't slide language.)

elena_slides.text: ELENA: “The regulatory framework provides for…” (Someone checks their phone. The words are correct and land nowhere.)

voices.text: Anna opens the floor. The room holds its breath for a moment.

choices.maya_line: MAYA, Zoe on her lap: “If there's room for her, there's room for every mother who isn't here tonight.”
choices.samir_line: SAMIR: “Three barriers between my home and this room — I counted them. Want the list?”
choices.silence: (Nobody from the routes speaks: the empty chairs speak instead.)

closing.text: ANNA, closing: “A proposal — before we repair anything, we map the cracks. All of them.” (The Crossroads unlocks for exploration: chapter 2 begins.)

start.text: The façades hold each other up. The people stopped.
A neighbourhood of padlocks nobody remembers the keys to.
You grow up fast here. Deciding, though, is always someone else's job.
The photos come out beautifully. The bills, less so.
The papers call it a problem. The people who live here call it home.
The water is close. Everything else is far.

You want to know what's missing? Ten people will have told you already, I imagine. (studying you) I'll tell you what used to be here: an after-school room, on Thursdays. They closed it “temporarily” three years ago.

choices.ask: “What would change if it reopened tomorrow?”
choices.silence: (Wait in silence.) “…And in any case, not one of the ten ever came back to tell me what they did with my answers.”

start.text: What's missing? Names on the doorbells. I deliver to twenty doors a week where I'm the only voice they hear. Write that down — if you're really writing.

choices.ask: “Who are they? Tell me one story.”
choices.silence: (Wait in silence.) “…Nobody ever waits for the second half of my answers. It's longer than the first.”

start.text: Ask me who stopped boarding the bus. Mrs Anh, evening shift, since they cut the 19:40. The two kids from the towers. The man with the crutch. My list is a timetable of absences.

choices.ask: “What would put them back on the bus?”
choices.silence: (Wait in silence.) “…You're the first one who didn't interrupt me. That's already something.”

start.text: (leafing through the empathy maps) Look: three neighbourhoods, three different ways of saying “nobody ever comes back”. That's where we start.

Elena convenes the first knowledge table. TOM: “They are not qualified.” ELENA: “They know what time the underpass turns frightening and which step breaks the shopping trolleys — none of our consultants knows that. THAT is qualification.”

choices.bring_maps: Bring the three empathy maps to the table.
choices.too_few: (You have fewer than three interviews: the table would be technicians talking to technicians.)

table.text: Viveca's Thursdays, Pablo's unnamed doorbells, Gwen's timetable of absences — read aloud, next to the budget sheets. TOM (after a silence): “…The step measurement. Where did you get it?” Each map becomes an amendment to one of this chapter's interventions.

too_few.text: ELENA: “Come back when you've really listened. A table with empty chairs on the experts' side is exactly the old way, with better wording.” (Mapping too little has a cost: the mission waits.)

P01.title: The Center Holds?
P01.description: Understand why the Community Center is losing its civic function.

objectives.talk_to_anna: Talk to Anna about the civic map.

P02.title: Same City, Different Routes
P02.description: Discover how the same district creates different routes for different people.

C01.title: Arriving Is Already Participation
C01.description: Investigate mobility barriers around the Crossroads.

N01.title: Listen Before Fixing
N01.description: Interview at least three people in different districts before proposing solutions.

objectives.interview_three: Listen to 3 people in different districts.

N02.title: The Crossing
N02.description: Make safe a crossing that blocks children, older people and disabled residents.

N03.title: Mandate, Not a Photo Op
N03.description: Turn political attention into a real commitment with clear responsibilities.

objectives.secure_mandate: Secure a commitment with defined responsibilities.

N04.title: Memory Is Not Nostalgia
N04.description: Use the neighbourhood's memory to legitimise the new instead of blocking it.

objectives.collect_memory: Gather memory and link it to a new proposal.

N05.title: The Repair Day
N05.description: Collective repair of a space with limited resources (Modular Repair mini-game).

N06.title: The Story Behind the Headline
N06.description: Choose whether Ruben fuels or defuses the false rumour.

objectives.provide_material: Provide authentic material for the story.

N07.title: From Rumor to Information
N07.description: Turn the newsstand into a reliable, multilingual info point.

objectives.setup_info: Set up the multilingual info point.

N08.title: Rules and Open Doors
N08.description: Find a legal way to open a common room (Commons Access mini-game).

objectives.agreement: Establish a use agreement with responsibilities.

N09.title: The Last Bus
N09.description: Document a missing connection and build a proposal.

N10.title: Care for the Carers
N10.description: Introduce rest practices and load sharing in the network.

N11.title: Nobody Is Off the Map
N11.description: Find isolated residents and connect them to a care network.

objectives.connect: Connect them to a care network.

N12.title: Mixed Tables
N12.description: Turn the bar into a meeting place for separate groups.

objectives.mixed_tables: Organise theme nights and a shared noticeboard.

N13.title: Care Made Visible
N13.description: Minimal green co-tended by residents, staying in their hands.

N14.title: Through a Child's Eyes
N14.description: Map the city from the children's point of view.

N15.title: Before the Water Rises
N15.description: Prepare networks and routes before the climate crisis (Crisis Response Map mini-game).

N16.title: Words to Belong
N16.description: Language and intergenerational memory course to truly participate.

N17.title: Don't Tell Our Story Without Us
N17.description: Counter-narrative co-created with residents and young people (Story Routes mini-game).

N18.title: The Offer
N18.description: Anti-quest: accept, negotiate or refuse the speculative offer.

objectives.decide_offer: Decide how to answer the offer.

E01.title: Experts of Everyday Life
E01.description: Bring three residents' empathy maps to the Town Hall knowledge table, alongside the technicians.

objectives.knowledge_table: Hold the first knowledge table with 3 empathy maps.

p1.title: Eurbania
p1.text: A mid-sized European city, like many others. Trams that are almost on time, squares that fill and empty, neighbours who nod without quite knowing each other's names.

p2.title: The Fragmentation
p2.text: Lately something has been pulling the city apart. Not a storm, not a crisis on the news — just distance. People retreating indoors, shared spaces going quiet, decisions made about everyone by fewer and fewer.

p3.title: The Community Center
p3.text: One place still tries to hold the threads together. Its doors are open, its noticeboard is full, and tonight it is busier than usual: the city has called an assembly, and someone has to show up.

p4.text: Five people could walk through that door. Tonight, one of them is you. Who will you be?

maya.tagline: A shift worker and Zoe's mother. For her the city is a puzzle of schedules that barely fit.

samir.tagline: A courier who knows every shortcut and every closed door.

elena.tagline: A Town Hall official who must turn listening into real decisions, without overpromising.

luca.tagline: A small business owner trying to serve the neighbourhood without going under.

custom.tagline: Your own route through Eurbania: choose who to help, knowing you can't help everyone.

heatwave.announce: Heat alert: 38° forecast. Isolated elders and fragile housing at risk in districts without shade. GIORGIA: “I have the Old Blocks vulnerable list… two years out of date.”
heatwave.reactive: Water gets handed out wherever. By evening an elderly man is taken to hospital — the logbook records his name.
heatwave.coordinated: The Center opens as a cool refuge, with shifts. MAYA (evening, tired): “Zoe did her homework in the courtyard today with six other kids. It's 36 degrees and it was… nice?”
heatwave.transformative: Pablo's door-to-door network and the neighbourhood cool rooms hold the day — the permanent “cool map” is born. PABLO: “Mine is from yesterday — twenty-three names, floor by floor. Let's go.”

rumor.announce: Viral post: “EXCLUSIVE: Community Center funds go only to SOME groups. Guess which ones.” SAMIR: “The ‘which ones’ is me, isn't it?”
rumor.reactive: A denial press release goes out. Nobody reads it.
rumor.coordinated: Public accounts on the noticeboard and an open clarification assembly. Trust steadies.
rumor.transformative: Abdullah's info kiosk publishes the numbers in five languages; Ruben signs the piece that dismantles the rumour with sources; Marta turns it into a comic strip on the wall. ABDULLAH: “The rumour ran. But this time the truth had a kiosk, and it was open.”

offer.announce: The Mystery Corporate Man turns up at the Town Hall with a rendering: “GREY YARDS SQUARE — opening in 90 days. Zero cost to the Council. Sign, and the problem disappears.” ALEXANDRIA: “Give me a credible alternative today.”
offer.reactive: A refusal on principle, with no alternative. The Offer will return, stronger.
offer.coordinated: Public conditions are negotiated: guaranteed hours, social tariffs. The commons hold, but don't grow.
offer.transformative: A civic stewardship pact answers the rendering: Tom's regulation, Sigrid's works, the traders' fund. The Corporate Man withdraws: “We'll be back when you're tired.”

closure.announce: Notice: “Temporary suspension: evening bus line 14 + Lake Edge after-school room. Resource rationalisation.” GWEN: “‘Temporary’. Like Viveca's Thursdays, three years ago.”
closure.reactive: A petition circulates. Signatures without data get filed away.
closure.coordinated: Gwen's real counts reach the committee with Elena. The line is saved on a reduced timetable.
closure.transformative: Three riders tell the committee what the last bus means — the resolution quotes their words. The after-school club reopens in the courtyard, Viveca volunteering. ZOE: “The new teacher is exactly as strict as the old one.”

flood.announce: Torrential rain over Lake Edge and Old Blocks. Teams, sandbags, assembly points: who goes to whom? BEN: “The new crossing is the only dry route for wheelchairs. I told you it wasn't ‘a detail’.”
flood.reactive: Pure emergency. A district sinks into fragmentation.
flood.coordinated: The networks activate; the damage is contained.
flood.transformative: The response becomes protocol — the “Water Plan” enters the civic pact. ANNA (that evening, in the half-flooded but crowded Center): “Look at yourselves — THIS is the network. Tomorrow we put it in writing.”

promises.promiseSaferCrossing: Make the crossing safe: temporary fix and the real proposal.
promises.promiseRepairDay: Run the Repair Day on courtyard 17 with crews and materials.
promises.promiseOpenInfoPoint: Set up the verified, multilingual info point at the newsstand.
promises.promiseOpenCommonRoom: Open the common room legally, clause by clause.
promises.promiseAccessibleBusInfo: Count a week of bus runs with Gwen and build the proposal.
promises.promiseChildFriendlyMeeting: Bring the children's map into a real meeting.
promises.promiseClimatePrep: Clear the channels, list the low houses, set the assembly point.
promises.promiseSupportLonelyElders: Build the door-to-door list with a face for every name.

assembly.intro: The big hall again. This time the map on the wall is yours: stories, promises, five days of crisis. Alexandria waits at the table — tonight the city decides its plan.
assembly.closing: No victory screen. The final shot is always the city map, re-coloured to its actual state. The Fragmentation has not vanished: it can be reduced, contained, crossed by new connections.

room.ben_ramp: Ben, looking at the new ramp at the entrance: "Better than last time."
room.ben_stairs: Ben: "Still stairs, still 18:00. You haven't listened to a thing."

commitment.alexandria: Alexandria: "I don't sign visions — I sign names, dates and reviews. Who does what, with which resources, by when — and how we'll know."

verifications.public_review: Public review in the big hall

story_viveca.title: Mrs Viveca's window
story_viveca.line: The courtyard she can see but not reach — her street, learned by heart, read aloud to the room.

story_pablo.title: Pablo's rounds
story_pablo.line: Nobody is off the map: Pablo's route proves care is already infrastructure — just unpaid.

story_gwen.title: Gwen and the last bus
story_gwen.line: When the last run is cut, it isn't a timetable that ends. It's Tuesdays at the market.

story_zoe.title: Zoe crosses the city
story_zoe.line: Maya arrived with Zoe. A city a child can cross alone is a city that keeps its promises.

story_samir_fence.title: The fence at the Crossroads
story_samir_fence.line: Samir said it out loud: a closed door doesn't ask who you are — it answers for you.

story_repair_day.title: The Repair Day
story_repair_day.line: Sigrid's ninety neighbours fixed more than objects: they fixed the habit of asking for help.

data_knowledge_table.title: The knowledge table
data_knowledge_table.line: Elena's table: three empathy maps side by side — experience, filed as evidence.

data_bus_counts.title: Ridership counts
data_bus_counts.line: Who boards, when, and who stopped boarding the day the evening run was cut.

data_map_holes.title: The map's blank spots
data_map_holes.line: Ben's survey of the crossing: where the official map says nothing, people fall through.

data_commission.title: The commission file
data_commission.line: Elena's commission: limits said out loud, procedures with names attached.

positions.synthesis: Sigrid: "So is ours: ninety neighbours." Put both offers on the same table
positions.residents: Side with the residents — the courtyard is not for sale
positions.evade: Move on and leave the offer on the table

positions.synthesis: A commission with a deadline: the resolution and the courtyard both hold
positions.urgency: Anna first — open now, paperwork later
positions.evade: Table it for the next session

positions.synthesis: Amin's way: the Center becomes one little table among many
positions.center: Keep the Center as the heart of the city

measures.m_open_courtyards: Open the courtyards to the neighbourhood
measures.m_common_room_hours: Evening hours for the common room
measures.m_safe_crossing: A safe crossing at the Crossroads
measures.m_youth_court: A youth court with a real mandate
measures.m_rumor_desk: A rumours desk — check before it spreads
measures.m_cool_rooms: Cool rooms for the next heatwave
measures.m_open_commission: An open commission with public minutes
measures.m_ramps_hours: Ramps first, then meeting times that work
measures.m_mutual_aid_roster: A mutual-aid roster for crisis days

thriving_network.title: Thriving Network
thriving_network.epilogue: The map lights up district by district — not in the same colour: each in its own. The Community Center is still there, but it is one light among many. Zoe crosses Ben's Crossing on her own. In Amin's bar, Tom explains Article 9 to the Youth Court kids. The Fragmentation has not vanished: it has become the visible seam along which the city has learned to stitch.
thriving_network.quote: Eurbania wasn't saved. It picked itself back up — which is another thing, and lasts longer.

fragile_progress.title: Fragile Progress
fragile_progress.epilogue: Some neighbourhoods breathe, others remain in shadow. The pact is signed, with tight margins and real reviews. Gwen drives the last run — saved, for this year.
fragile_progress.quote: This is not the ending. It is the point where the story decides to go on.

institutional_capture.title: Institutional Capture
institutional_capture.epilogue: The plan exists: bound, filed, quoted in press releases. The spaces have new plaques and office hours. Anna moves the meetings to Amin's bar — later and later, smaller and smaller.
institutional_capture.quote: The city won a document. The people lost a place.

commons_without_voice.title: Commons Without Voice
commons_without_voice.epilogue: The courtyards are open, the walls painted, the plants tended. But the same faces are missing from every opening-day photo. Samir looks at the full room and counts the right chairs with the wrong names.
commons_without_voice.quote: A more colourful city is not yet a more just city.

resistance.title: Resistance
resistance.epilogue: The council votes down the pact. The network does not dissolve: it tightens. Pablo's rounds, Siobhan's channels, Amin's mixed tables — everything holds, without stamps, with effort. Elena, at the Town Hall, keeps a drawer full of drafts at the ready.
resistance.quote: Vulnerable, autonomous, alive. Just waiting for the institution to come knocking again — politely, this time.

fragmented.title: Fragmented
fragmented.epilogue: No collapse: only frictions that win. The big hall empty again, the noticeboard frozen, the Corporate's rendering hanging where Anna's map used to be. People still greet each other — from a distance. The map goes dark neighbourhood by neighbourhood, without a sound.
fragmented.quote: The Fragmentation didn't win a battle. It won by habit.

modular_repair.title: Modular Repair — one Saturday
modular_repair.brief: Sigrid: "Budget: near zero; materials: what you see; time: one Saturday. A lot can be done, if nobody demands perfect."
